extraction.one_line_summary: DENNIS Rowan won the 18.5km technical TT by 5 seconds over Cavagna, while YATES Adam's unexpected 7th place (only 7sec behind Almeida) reshuffled GC dynamics.
tactical_patterns: Dennis exploited technical corners and final 1500m push where pure power specialists like Cavagna lose seconds disproportionately.
Ineos positioned both Porte and A.Yates within 7sec of leader Almeida, creating numerical advantage for mountain stages.
Almeida prioritized TT gains knowing his 30-40sec deficit pattern to Yates on gradual climbs vs steeper finishes.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Only 7 seconds behind Almeida in 18.5km TT despite losing 30+ to him in UAE Tour 13km TT reveals form progression
rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: positive: First win since 2019 Worlds, maximized technical course advantage through corner speed differential over pure power riders
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: Beat GC rivals as expected but margin over A.Yates compressed compared to UAE Tour, vulnerable on upcoming climbs
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémy: negative: Lost 5 seconds to Dennis despite being stage favorite, technical descents exposed weakness relative to rolling power courses
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: Lost 55 seconds creating 1min gap to teammate Kruijswijk, complicates Jumbo's dual-leader strategy for mountain stages
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: Only 35sec to Dennis post-Paris-Nice crash indicates hidden form, 6sec behind leader with Yates as co-leader
